column1: # gruntz match baseline - generated by gruntz.match.status. DO NOT hand-edit.
#   units rows:     unit  n_functions  matched   (matched = at 100% fuzzy now;
#                    the README's per-module 'exact' uses objdiff's byte-exact count)
#   function rows:  unit  function  best_pct  cur_pct  tries  src_hash  rva
#   rva      = retail RVA (stable identity): a vanished row whose rva is still
#              occupied is a rename, not a loss - so signature edits don't regress.
#              It is ALSO what gates best_pct: the rva identifies the BODY, and a
#              best measures a body, not a name (see status.rebound()).
#   best_pct = best-ever (max) objdiff fuzzy% - the REGRESSION gate (may be 100%).
#              A RATCHET: for a given rva it never decreases. Only an explicit
#              `update --accept-regressions`, or the rva MOVING (the name now labels
#              a different body), may lower it. Editing a function does NOT - that is
#              exactly when the high-water must hold (structure over current %).
#   cur_pct  = fuzzy% at THIS commit - `diff <revA> [<revB>]` two commits to see
#              the moves (e.g. unit 5->10 fns, a fn 10%->40%) while best_pct holds.
#   tries    = times this function's source fingerprint changed (how much it has
#              been worked on - high = hard to match).
#   src_hash = per-function source fingerprint. Drives tries + the TOUCHED/REGRESS
#              split ONLY; it must never gate best_pct (it used to, and eroded it).
# Query: python -m gruntz.match.status check | status | summary | diff <revA> [<revB>]
